task_completion.description: The MRHead sample dataset is loaded and visible in the slice views.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The standard MRHead brain MRI volume is displayed in the Red, Yellow, and Green slice views.

task_completion.description: Anatomical orientation markers are visible in the slice view corners.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The letters 'A', 'P', 'R', 'L', 'S', or 'I' are clearly visible in the corners of the slice views (e.g., 'R' on the right side, 'L' on the left).

task_completion.description: Patient name information is not visible in the slice views.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The corners of the slice views do not display any text resembling a patient name (e.g., 'Anonymous', 'MRHead', or specific names). The area typically reserved for this is empty.

task_completion.description: Study date and time information is not visible in the slice views.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The corners of the slice views do not display any date or timestamp text.

task_completion.description: The annotation settings are applied consistently across all three standard views.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Red (Axial), Yellow (Sagittal), and Green (Coronal) views all show orientation markers and lack patient/date information simultaneously.

integrity.description: The agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to configure settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the 'Application Settings', 'Slice View Annotations' module, or slice controller popups, rather than purely Python console commands.

integrity.description: The screenshots depict the actual 3D Slicer application interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface elements (toolbar, module panel, slice viewers) match the standard 3D Slicer layout.

integrity.description: The annotations are rendered by the software, not added via image editing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The orientation letters appear in the standard Slicer font and position within the viewports, consistent with the software's rendering style.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The MRHead.nrrd dataset is a public sample that does not contain DICOM metadata for 'Patient Name' or 'Study Date'. Therefore, these fields will appear empty in the slice views regardless of whether the specific visibility setting is toggled off or if the data is just missing. The VLM should verify that no text is visible for these fields. Orientation markers (A, P, R, L, S, I) are the primary visual confirmation of the configuration.
